behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the handoff between taxonomy source/config and request-serialization/API-client modules. A pure unit test of a payload helper can miss wiring bugs where only a subset is passed at call time. Rejecting the bogus shortcut "pick Unit because mocks exist": this behavior is about what is actually sent across a module boundary ("request sent to the external API"). Higher levels are not required because no real OS/network behavior or model judgment is needed; a local stub transport that captures outbound payload is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real classifier module with a stub API client that records outbound requests. For multiple taxonomy fixtures, assert each dispatched request contains the full configured label list (no missing/extra/reordered-loss issues as defined by contract).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected System/Agentic using behavior text: this checks request composition only, not live network effects or model-output correctness.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is a local decision rule: membership check against taxonomy and fallback mapping. MOCK-MISS does not force module wiring; the real failure can be reproduced at one function/object boundary with deterministic inputs. Rejecting the bogus shortcut "Integration by default for multi-module features": behavior text focuses on output mapping rule, not transport/runtime interaction. Higher levels are disqualified because no real infra or model variability is needed to trigger invalid-label fallback.
behaviors.test_description: Call the response-normalization/validation function with out-of-taxonomy labels (case/whitespace variants as applicable) and assert result is exactly "unknown".
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected Integration because behavior can be fully exercised without module handoff; rejected Agentic/System since no live API/OS dependency is required.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Determinism/idempotence of normalization is pure local logic (parsing/canonicalization/mapping). MOCK-MISS: no cross-module interaction is required to expose this failure. Rejecting bogus shortcut "raise level because broader feature calls AI": behavior text is explicitly about identical raw responses and the normalization step.
behaviors.test_description: Feed the same raw response object/string repeatedly into normalization and assert identical final label each run; include property-style repetition over representative fixtures.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected Integration/Agentic by behavior text: scope is normalization determinism for fixed input, not live call behavior.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on repeated live AI API outcomes. By LLM-DEP, correctness depends on real model responses and their variability; mocks would hide this class of failure. Rejecting bogus shortcut "Integration with stubbed responses": stubs cannot test whether real model decisions drift while provider response is unchanged. Not Workflow because this is a single agentic decision repeated, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run N real classification calls with identical document+taxonomy, store both provider raw response and module final label, and assert label changes only when provider response differs. Track flake rate over repeated runs.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected Integration/System due explicit "live API calls" and dependence on model-returned content; rejected Workflow because no sequential multi-agent stages.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at policy-to-dispatch boundary: token-budget logic must control whether/how request is emitted. A unit test of truncation alone can miss wiring where over-budget requests are still dispatched. Rejecting bogus shortcut "Unit because algorithm is local": behavior text includes "before dispatch," which is a module interaction boundary. Higher levels not needed if tokenizer and dispatcher can be in-process test doubles.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier with real request builder and a capturing stub client. Use over/under budget documents and assert policy outcome (truncate or reject) is applied and dispatch behavior matches configuration (sent modified request or not sent).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected System/Agentic using behavior text: this validates pre-dispatch enforcement, not live network/model output quality.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This requires real provider accounting from a real AI API call; mocking token usage can trivially pass while hiding tokenizer/model-side differences. By LLM-DEP rule, execution path through real AI API defaults to Agentic unless exclusively testing wrapper plumbing. Here the assertion is about provider-reported usage itself, so higher-than-Integration is required. Not Workflow: single call metric, no chained model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live calls with representative documents and read provider-reported usage fields; assert observed usage <= configured budget (with explicit tolerance/contract if provider rounds).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected Integration with concrete disqualifier: behavior text says "Provider-reported token usage," which cannot be validated faithfully via stubs.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is retry-orchestration across classifier, backoff policy, and API client boundary. In-process stub returning rate-limit errors plus controllable clock can reproduce it; real OS/network behavior is not strictly required. Rejecting bogus shortcut "System because it mentions API": behavior focuses on control-flow handling of a known signal, not network pathologies. No model-output dependence, so Agentic/Workflow are unnecessary.
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub API client that returns rate-limit error then success; use fake clock/sleeper to assert wait duration and retry count, and assert caller receives successful label instead of immediate error.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected System by behavior text: requirement is logical handling of provider signal (wait+retry), which is reproducible with deterministic in-process doubles.
